commits: ceffaad: docs(0.5.2): NAME-aliases implementation design memo

tests.note: 1 xpassed — the pre-existing xfail tests/test_config_consistency.py::TestAliasTableCoverage::test_all_model_names_resolve_to_self (strict=False) now resolves correctly thanks to the collision-safe loader; reported as xpassed, not a failure.

additional_changes_made_in_scope: Config anchors drifted as new entries were inserted (expected per brief); all edits were anchored on entry bodies, not line numbers, so no manual re-grep adjustment was needed.

NOTE for orchestrator: tests/test_config_consistency.py loads TWO configs — the existing TestAliasTableCoverage/TestFallbackConsistency suites read airlock/cli/templates/config.yaml (the simpler CLI template, NOT in this pack's MODIFY set, left untouched), while the NEW reference-integrity + batch-key tests read the deployed root config.yaml. The template is intentionally not updated here (out of scope); template-based tests still pass.

capability.py was kept minimal (airlock_provider_for + normalize_provider_token only). endpoints_for/capability_record/model_info shown in design note §4.5 are CAP-modelinfo's surface and were deliberately NOT added.
agent_verify_result: pass — targeted: 246 passed, 1 xpassed, 0 failed. Adjacent regression (monitor/batch/guardian/enhanced/transparency/local-vllm): 212 passed. Full suite: 2237 passed, 107 skipped, 1 xpassed, 1 pre-existing unrelated failure (tests/test_fathom_init.py::test_init_engine_with_fathomdb — fails by design because optional fathomdb is not installed; no import of capability/model_alias/router).
